persons.arguments_against: The dossier flags this as a single-initial namesake trap, but that flag is mechanical: the staff-list name is the full distinctive forename 'Saul', identical to the biography's printed forename, not a bare initial. The realistic counter-hypothesis is a relative or successor Samuel holding the Postmaster-Generalship of New South Wales in 1879-80. That fails on three independent dimensions at once: the biography states verbatim he was 'Late postmaster-general of New South Wales' (exact office and colony); the stint carries C.M.G., which the biography dates to 1874 and which he held until his 1882 K.C.M.G. — exactly the honour state expected in 1879-80; and the stint ends in 1880, the very year the biography has him leave for London as agent-general for New South Wales. A second Saul Samuel, C.M.G., serving as NSW Postmaster-General in the same two years is not a live possibility.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second Saul Samuel, C.M.G., in New South Wales public life in 1879-80, or evidence the biography subject had already left the Postmaster-Generalship before 1879 with a different Samuel succeeding him under the same forename.

notes: Exact office+colony+honour+chronology agreement; the stint's end in 1880 dovetails with the biography's agent-general appointment in London in 1880.
